name: Arrhenius Plot for Activation Energy
description: Extract the activation energy and pre-exponential factor by measuring rate constants at several temperatures, linearizing the Arrhenius equation as ln k versus 1/T, and reading the activation energy from the slope and the pre-factor from the intercept.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Rate constants at many T"]
    N2["k = A e^(−Ea/RT)"]
    N3["Linearize: ln k vs 1/T"]
    N4["Plot the data"]
    N5{"Plot linear?"}
    N6["Single Arrhenius regime"]
    N7["Non-Arrhenius curvature"]
    N8["Slope = −Ea/R"]
    N9["Intercept = ln A"]
    N10["Report Ea and A"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|yes| N6
    N6 --> N8
    N5 -->|no| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Über die Reaktionsgeschwindigkeit bei der Inversion von Rohrzucker durch Säuren
Physical Chemistry, 11th ed. — Temperature Dependence of Reaction Rates
sources.authors: Atkins, P.; de Paula, J.; Keeler, J.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

nodeDetails.label: Rate constants at many T
nodeDetails.detail: k measured over a temperature range
